function.name: calculate
function.description: Calculate the result of a mathematical expression.

expression.description: The mathematical expression to calculate, such as '2 + 2'. The expression can contain numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), parentheses, and spaces.

function.name: cancel_pending_order
function.description: Cancel a pending order. If the order is already processed or delivered, it cannot be cancelled. The agent needs to explain the cancellation detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed. If the user confirms, the order status will be changed to 'cancelled' and the payment will be refunded. The refund will be added to the user's gift card balance immediately if the payment was made using a gift card, otherwise the refund would take 5-7 business days to process. The function returns the order details after the cancellation.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

reason.description: The reason for cancellation, which should be either 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake'.

function.name: exchange_delivered_order_items
function.description: Exchange items in a delivered order to new items of the same product type. For a delivered order, return or exchange can be only done once by the agent. The agent needs to explain the exchange detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

item_ids.description: The item ids to be exchanged, each such as '1008292230'. There could be duplicate items in the list.

new_item_ids.description: The item ids to be exchanged for, each such as '1008292230'. There could be duplicate items in the list. Each new item id should match the item id in the same position and be of the same product.

payment_method_id.description: The payment method id to pay or receive refund for the item price difference, such as 'gift_card_0000000' or 'credit_card_0000000'. These can be looked up from the user or order details.

function.name: find_user_id_by_email
function.description: Find user id by email. If the user is not found, the function will return an error message.

email.description: The email of the user, such as 'something@example.com'.

function.name: find_user_id_by_name_zip
function.description: Find user id by first name, last name, and zip code. If the user is not found, the function will return an error message. By default, find user id by email, and only call this function if the user is not found by email or cannot remember email.

first_name.description: The first name of the customer, such as 'John'.

last_name.description: The last name of the customer, such as 'Doe'.

zip.description: The zip code of the customer, such as '12345'.

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get the status and details of an order.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

function.name: get_product_details
function.description: Get the inventory details of a product.

product_id.description: The product id, such as '6086499569'. Be careful the product id is different from the item id.

function.name: get_user_details
function.description: Get the details of a user, including their orders.

function.name: list_all_product_types
function.description: List the name and product id of all product types. Each product type has a variety of different items with unique item ids and options. There are only 50 product types in the store.

function.name: modify_pending_order_address
function.description: Modify the shipping address of a pending order. The agent needs to explain the modification detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

address1.description: The first line of the address, such as '123 Main St'.

address2.description: The second line of the address, such as 'Apt 1' or ''.

function.name: modify_pending_order_items
function.description: Modify items in a pending order to new items of the same product type. For a pending order, this function can only be called once. The agent needs to explain the exchange detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

item_ids.description: The item ids to be modified, each such as '1008292230'. There could be duplicate items in the list.

new_item_ids.description: The item ids to be modified for, each such as '1008292230'. There could be duplicate items in the list. Each new item id should match the item id in the same position and be of the same product.

payment_method_id.description: The payment method id to pay or receive refund for the item price difference, such as 'gift_card_0000000' or 'credit_card_0000000'. These can be looked up from the user or order details.

function.name: modify_pending_order_payment
function.description: Modify the payment method of a pending order. The agent needs to explain the modification detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

payment_method_id.description: The payment method id to pay or receive refund for the item price difference, such as 'gift_card_0000000' or 'credit_card_0000000'. These can be looked up from the user or order details.

function.name: modify_user_address
function.description: Modify the default address of a user. The agent needs to explain the modification detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed.

address1.description: The first line of the address, such as '123 Main St'.

address2.description: The second line of the address, such as 'Apt 1' or ''.

function.name: return_delivered_order_items
function.description: Return some items of a delivered order. The order status will be changed to 'return requested'. The agent needs to explain the return detail and ask for explicit user confirmation (yes/no) to proceed. The user will receive follow-up email for how and where to return the item.

order_id.description: The order id, such as '#W0000000'. Be careful there is a '#' symbol at the beginning of the order id.

item_ids.description: The item ids to be returned, each such as '1008292230'. There could be duplicate items in the list.

payment_method_id.description: The payment method id to pay or receive refund for the item price difference, such as 'gift_card_0000000' or 'credit_card_0000000'. These can be looked up from the user or order details.

function.name: think
function.description: Use the tool to think about something. It will not obtain new information or change the database, but just append the thought to the log. Use it when complex reasoning or some cache memory is needed.

function.name: transfer_to_human_agents
function.description: Transfer the user to a human agent, with a summary of the user's issue. Only transfer if the user explicitly asks for a human agent, or if the user's issue cannot be resolved by the agent with the available tools.

summary.description: A summary of the user's issue.

reason_matrix: No self-edge: the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
Output of Calculate (a numeric result) is neither an order_id nor a valid reason enum; it does not feed into or control cancel_pending_order.
Exchange requires order_id/item_ids/new_item_ids/payment_method_id; Calculate’s numeric result does not provide or influence these inputs.
FindUserIdByEmail is an entry-point lookup and cannot have incoming edges; Calculate’s output is not an email anyway.
FindUserIdByNameZip is an entry-point lookup and cannot have incoming edges; Calculate’s output isn’t name/zip data.
get_order_details needs an order_id string; Calculate’s result does not supply or influence it.
get_product_details needs a product_id; Calculate’s result does not provide or meaningfully affect this input.
get_user_details needs a user_id; Calculate’s result does not provide or influence it.
list_all_product_types takes no inputs; Calculate’s output is not used and does not directly drive its execution in a defined scenario.
modify_pending_order_address needs structured address fields; a numeric calculation result does not map to these inputs or control execution.
modify_pending_order_items needs order_id/item ids/new item ids/payment_method_id; Calculate’s result does not supply or influence them.
modify_pending_order_payment needs order_id and payment_method_id; Calculate’s result does not provide or influence these IDs.
modify_user_address needs user_id and address fields; Calculate’s result does not map to these inputs or control execution.
return_delivered_order_items needs order_id/item_ids/payment_method_id; Calculate’s result does not provide or influence these inputs.
Think accepts a free-form thought string; the numeric result from Calculate can be embedded as part of that input (e.g., logging a computed total), satisfying the ‘part of input’ criterion.
transfer_to_human_agents accepts a summary string; the calculated result can be included in the summary (e.g., computed refund amount), so Calculate’s output can be part of the input.
No data from a cancelled order is needed to evaluate a math expression, so there is no input/output linkage.
Self-edge is not allowed; the target function cannot be its own neighbor.
If cancellation fails because the order is delivered, the returned status guides an exchange flow and the order_id from the cancel result can be reused as input.
Entry-point user lookup; it does not consume order details from a cancellation outcome.
Entry-point user lookup by name/zip; it does not consume cancellation output.
After cancelling, we may fetch fresh order details using the order_id included in the cancel response to confirm status/refund.
Requires a product_id and is unrelated to cancellation; cancel output does not provide or necessitate a product lookup.
If the payment was a gift card, the cancel response (order details, including user_id/payment info) can prompt fetching user details to verify updated gift card balance.
Listing product types is unrelated and does not consume cancellation output.
Once cancelled, the order is not pending; the cancel result precludes modification rather than triggers it, so this is not a follow-on call.
Same as address: a cancelled/non-pending order cannot be modified; no positive follow-on from cancel output.
Same reasoning: after cancellation there is nothing to modify; cancel output would stop, not trigger, this call.
Updating a user’s default address is not driven by the cancellation result and does not consume its output.
If cancellation is impossible because the order is delivered, the cancel outcome guides a return flow and the order_id/items from the cancel response can be reused.
This thinking tool does not take structured input from the cancel result and is not operationally required after cancellation.
If cancellation fails or the user insists, the cancel outcome informs the decision to transfer and its details can be included in the summary input.
No direct data dependency; the exchange returns order/order-item updates while Calculate only accepts a math expression string.
Applies to pending orders; after an exchange on a delivered order, cancellation is not applicable and no outputs feed into it.
No self edge; a function is not considered its own neighbour.
User ID lookup by email is an entry-point and does not take outputs from an exchange.
Name+ZIP user lookup is an entry-point and not driven by exchange outputs.
Post-exchange, the agent may retrieve the updated order status/details using the returned order_id.
Requires a product_id, which the exchange result does not provide directly; inventory checks would be pre-exchange.
Exchange outcome (e.g., refund/charge details) can prompt fetching user details (and user_id can come from the exchanged order details), influencing this call.
Catalog listing is independent and does not use exchange outputs.
Only valid for pending orders; exchange handles delivered orders, so outputs don't apply.
Pending-order item modification is unrelated to a delivered-order exchange result.
Pending-order payment modification is unrelated to the exchange result.
Changing default user address is not a follow-on from the exchange and does not use its outputs.
Return and exchange are mutually exclusive for a delivered order; after executing an exchange you cannot initiate a return.
The Think tool takes a free-form thought and has no structured input dependency on exchange results.
If the exchange fails or is blocked by policy, that result can trigger escalation and its details can be used in the transfer summary.
calculate takes a math expression; the user_id or error from FindUserIdByEmail is not used or influential.
cancel_pending_order requires an order_id; the target returns a user_id/error, so it is not used as input.
exchange_delivered_order_items requires order_id/item_ids/payment_method_id; the target’s user_id is not part of the input.
self-edge is not allowed; the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
If email lookup fails (error output), we fall back to name+zip lookup; the target’s output directly triggers calling this function.
get_order_details requires an order_id; the target’s user_id is not used.
get_product_details requires a product_id; the target’s user_id is not used.
get_user_details requires a user_id; the target returns user_id, so its output is the direct input here.
list_all_product_types has no inputs; the target’s output does not affect its execution.
modify_pending_order_address requires an order_id; the target’s user_id is not used as input.
modify_pending_order_items requires an order_id; the target’s user_id is not used as input.
modify_pending_order_payment requires an order_id; the target’s user_id is not used as input.
modify_user_address requires a user_id; the target returns user_id, so its output is directly consumed.
return_delivered_order_items requires an order_id; the target’s user_id is not used as input.
think only takes a free-form thought and does not depend on the target’s output.
If the email lookup fails and the issue can’t proceed, that error outcome can lead to transferring to a human agent, so the target’s output influences execution.
The user_id returned is not a mathematical expression and cannot be used as input to Calculate.
CancelPendingOrder requires order_id and reason; the user_id from the target is not an input and only indirectly helps find orders.
ExchangeDeliveredOrderItems takes order_id/item ids/payment_method_id; it does not use user_id directly.
FindUserIdByEmail is an entry-point lookup by email and should not receive inputs from other functions; it also doesn’t take user_id.
No self-edge: the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
GetOrderDetails needs an order_id; the user_id output is not used directly as input.
GetProductDetails needs a product_id; it does not use the user_id.
GetUserDetails requires user_id, which is exactly the output of the target function—direct dependency.
ListAllProductTypes takes no inputs and is independent of user identification.
ModifyPendingOrderAddress requires order_id and address fields; it does not use user_id directly.
ModifyPendingOrderItems requires order_id/item ids/payment_method_id; it does not use user_id.
ModifyPendingOrderPayment requires order_id/payment_method_id; it does not use user_id.
ModifyUserAddress takes user_id and address details; it directly uses the target function’s output.
ReturnDeliveredOrderItems requires order_id/item ids/payment_method_id; it does not use user_id.
Think accepts a free-form thought string and is not directly influenced by a user_id lookup result.
If the name+zip lookup fails (error output), that failure can trigger transferring to a human agent, so the target function’s outcome directly influences executing this function.
No dependency on order details; math evaluation is unrelated to order status/items.
Order details provide status (must be pending) and the same order_id, so the result directly governs whether cancellation can proceed.
Order details confirm delivered status and provide item/payment info needed to set up an exchange.
Entry lookup function; acts as an entry point and doesn’t take order details as input.
Entry lookup function; acts as an entry point and doesn’t take order details as input.
No self edge allowed; the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
Order details include items/product references; those product_ids can be used to fetch inventory details.
Order details typically include the user_id; that user_id can be used to fetch full user details including payment methods.
Listing product types doesn’t require or use order details and isn’t directly driven by them.
Requires pending status and order_id; order details determine eligibility and provide current address context.
Requires pending status, item_ids, and payment method; order details determine eligibility and provide items/payment info.
Requires pending status and a payment method; order details determine eligibility and expose current payment method choices.
User_id from order details can be used to update the user’s default address if the order reveals an outdated address.
Requires delivered status and item_ids/payment method; order details determine eligibility and provide needed inputs.
Reasoning/logging tool; not a business action influenced by order details.
If order details show a case the agent cannot resolve (e.g., policy constraints), that outcome can drive a transfer to a human agent.
No dependency: calculating a math expression doesn’t consume or depend on product inventory details.
Cancellation uses only order_id and a reason; product details are neither inputs nor required to decide cancellation.
Yes. New item_ids must be of the same product and in stock; these IDs are typically chosen from the product’s inventory/variants returned by GetProductDetails.
No. It’s an entry-point lookup by email; product details are unrelated and this function shouldn’t have incoming edges from product data.
No. Another entry-point lookup (name+zip); unrelated to product info and not driven by product details.
No. Order details are fetched by order_id; product inventory data does not feed into this call.
No. Self-edge is disallowed; the target function cannot be its own neighbour.
No. Requires a user_id and returns user info; product details aren’t used here.
No. Listing all product types precedes or is independent of fetching a specific product’s details; the output of GetProductDetails isn’t used to call it.
No. Changing a pending order’s address doesn’t involve product inventory information.
Yes. Choosing valid replacement items for a pending order requires knowing the same product’s available item_ids/variants from GetProductDetails.
No. Payment method changes depend on order/payment info, not on product inventory details.
No. Updating a user’s default address is unrelated to product inventory data.
No. Returning delivered items needs item_ids from the order, not product inventory for a product type.
No. “Think” takes an arbitrary thought; product details aren’t required as input and don’t directly drive its execution.

Yes. If product details reveal an issue the agent cannot resolve (e.g., missing/invalid product or constraints), the agent may transfer and include those details in the summary, making product data part of the input and influencing the decision to execute the transfer.
Independent math evaluation; it does not consume or depend on the user details returned by GetUserDetails.

We can take an order_id from the user's orders returned by GetUserDetails and fetch its detailed status.
Requires product_id, which is not reliably provided by GetUserDetails (it typically exposes orders/item_ids), so there is no direct input dependency.
